highlighted_text: Although System 2 believes itself to be where the action is, the automatic System 1 is the hero of the book.
note: Reframe my mental model — most of my decisions aren't deliberate; they're System 1.

highlighted_text: When you concentrate, your pupils dilate; when the task is finished, they contract back to their resting state.
We found that people who were given a difficult mental task could not see things that were perfectly visible just outside their focus of attention.
note: Inattentional blindness — the gorilla experiment. Use this when explaining why context-switching is so costly.

highlighted_text: Activities that impose high demands on System 2 require self-control, and the exertion of self-control is depleting and unpleasant.
note: Decision fatigue is real and measurable.

highlighted_text: Our minds are constantly making associations between words, ideas, and experiences.
